fault_hypothesis: The overlap check on line 76 incorrectly allows adjacent intervals by using '>=' and '<=' comparisons. This causes intervals that touch at their boundaries to be considered non-overlapping, when the business logic requires them to be rejected.

constraints.intent: Adjacent intervals should be rejected, so the non-overlap condition must use strict inequalities.
The new interval must be strictly separated from every existing interval checked so far.

spec.expr: all(start__1 != e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: A new booking cannot start exactly when an existing booking ends.

spec.expr: not __cbfl_result[0] or all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If an interval is accepted, it must be strictly separated from all original bookings.
